How to check the version: Turn on the power of XDJ-RX3. Press the [MENU(UTILITY)] button for over 1 second to open the [UTILITY] screen. Scroll the screen by turning the rotary selector to show the version number on the bottom of the screen. Press the [MENU(UTILITY)] button to close the [UTILITY] screen. Preparation for update: Unzip the .zip file on your computer. Unzip the downloaded [XDJ-RX3_v***.zip] file to find the following update file: XDJRX3.UPD Copy the unzipped update file to the root directory of a USB storage device. * DO NOT rename the file name. * Please use a USB storage device (USB Mass Storage Class-compliant) formatted in FAT or FAT32. How to update: Enter the update mode: Ensure that XDJ-RX3 is powered off and no USB storage device and USB cable is connected. Then, turn on the power while pressing both the [USB1 STOP] and [RELOOP/EXIT] buttons simultaneously. When the ”Connect USB storage device into top USB1 port.” message is displayed, please release your finger from the buttons. Insert the USB storage device containing the copied update file into the USB1 port on the right side. DO NOT insert the USB storage device or other USB device into the USB2 port. The update starts automatically and the Progress Bar is displayed. Both the current and the updated versions are shown there simultaneously. * DO NOT remove the USB storage device or turn off the power during the update. When the update is finished, the below message is displayed: ”Firmware update is complete. Turn the power off/on before using.” When the complete message is displayed, turn the power off. Follow "How to check the version" to check the firmware version to make sure that the firmware has been updated. Then disconnect the USB storage device. If the update process does not start or "MAIN update file is corrupt." is displayed, the downloaded file might be corrupted. In that case, start over the update process again from downloading the update file. If the update process still fails even after that, please try another USB storage device because the USB device could cause the failure. If the Progress Bar does not progress and the update does not complete during updating or "MAIN firmware update failed" or other error message is displayed, please contact the Customer Support.

Firmware Update Procedure Please download the firmware from the Pioneer DJ website and copy it to a USB flash drive for the update. USB flash drive to be used for the update: Please use a USB flash drive (Mass Storage Class) formatted in FAT or FAT32. Extract the Firmware File Unzip the downloaded file "DJM-TOUR1_xxx.zip" to find the following file. DJM-TOUR1_xxx.upd - “xxx” is the version of the new firmware. - Extension (.upd) might not be shown depending on your computer settings. Preparations for updating firmware Copy the firmware file (DJM-TOUR1_xxx.upd) to the root directory of the above prepared USB flash drive. - Do not change the name of the extracted files. - Ensure that only one update file is saved in a USB flash drive when you update the firmware. Program Update Procedures 1. How to enter the Update mode. Turn on the power while pressing both [ON/OFF (BEAT FX)] and [MID (FX FREQUENCY)] buttons. - Please disconnect all devices and cables from USB, LINK and INTERNET terminals. 2. Check the current version of your firmware on the display. If current version is displayed as “xxx”, you do not need the update process because it is the latest firmware. 3. Insert the USB flash drive containing the firmware files into the USB port. The update process will begin and the progress bar and percentage will be displayed on the screen. - Do not turn the power off or disconnect the USB flash drive during the update. - The update will be completed in approximately 9 minutes. 4. When the message [COMPLETE] appears, the update is completed. Please turn the power off and disconnect the USB flash drive. If the firmware update will not start or an error occurred during the firmware update, the downloaded file might be broken. Please download the firmware again. FAQ for the Important Notice Regarding Your Product: Request for Updating the Firmware on DJ Multi Player CDJ-3000 announced on Dec 3, 2024. 1. Are there any issues when using the unit with DJ applications like rekordbox? This issue is caused by communication between parts of the product and doesn't affect how it works with DJ software. Please use rekordbox or a certified application. rekordbox｜DJ software for professional DJs AlphaTheta Certification Program (formerly Pioneer DJ Certification Program) 2. Is it OK to use CDJ-3000 units with different firmware versions at the same time? Please update the product firmware to the latest version on all your CDJ-3000 units as soon as possible. 3. Should I update the firmware if I haven’t experienced any problems so far? Please update the product firmware to the latest version on all your CDJ-3000 units as soon as possible. 4. How long does a firmware update take? The update will take a few minutes to complete. *Please don't turn off the power during the firmware update. 5. If the firmware update fails, can I install the version 3.16 firmware if I have it? If the firmware update fails and the product starts in Update mode, try updating the firmware again. For details, refer to the Firmware Update Guide . If nothing appears on the screen when you start up the product, please contact our customer support centre. 6. I'm using this CDJ-3000 with another one that has firmware version 3.16. Should I match the firmware so both are using 3.16? Please update the product firmware to the latest version on all your CDJ-3000 units as soon as possible.

About the Firmware Update Procedure Please download the firmware update from the AlphaTheta Support website and use a USB memory. To create a USB memory for the update : A USB memory device must be formatted in FAT or FAT32 (a device that belongs to the USB mass storage class). How to check if your CDJ-3000 requires the firmware update Turn on the power of CDJ-3000. Press [MENU / UTILITY] and hold for over 1 second. Then [UTILITY] screen is displayed. The firmware VERSION No. is displayed on the upper right of the screen. Press [MENU / UTILITY] to return to the menu screen. Extract the Firmware File Unzip the downloaded file"CDJ3000_v***.zip". The below file will appear. CDJ3Kv***.UPD (*.***,*** bytes) Please refer to the latest information on the support website for asterisks. Preparations for updating the firmware Copy the extracted firmware files to the root directory of a USB memory device. Do not change the name of the extracted files. Ensure that only one update file is saved in a USB flash drive when you update the firmware. Use a USB device that is formatted in FAT or FAT32 (mass storage class). Program Update Procedures 1. Please set your CDJ player in the status below: Power off. No device connected (USB memory, SD Card, LINK, or Computer). 2. Turn on the power on your CDJ Player by pressing the both "IN/CUE" and "RELOOP/EXIT" buttons. 3. A message [Connect the USB storage device to the USB port] appears on the display. Then release both buttons. 4. Insert the USB memory device containing the firmware files into the USB port. The update process will begin and a progress bar will be displayed on the screen. Do not turn the power off during the update. The update will be completed in approx. 1 minutes. The following message appears when the update is completed: [Firmware update is completed. Turn the power off before using] 5. Turn the power off and remove the USB memory device after the update has been completed. If the progress indicator does not progress during updating and the update completed message does not appear after 10 minutes, or if the message [MAIN firmware update failed] or [PANEL firmware update failed] appears, contact our customer support center.

About the Firmware Update Procedure Please download the firmware update from the AlphaTheta Support website and use a USB memory. To create a USB memory for the update : A USB memory device must be formatted in FAT or FAT32 (a device belongs to the USB mass storage class) How to check if your CDJ-2000 requires the firmware update 1. Turn on the power of CDJ-2000. 2. Press [MENU / UTILITY] and hold for over 1 second. Then [UTILITY] screen is displayed. 3. Scroll the screen menu by a rotary selector, firmware VERSION No. will be displayed. 4. Press [MENU / UTILITY] to return to the menu screen. Extract the Firmware File Unzip the downloaded file"CDJ-2000v***.zip". Four files will appear. C2KMAIN.UPD (*.***,*** bytes) C2KGUI.UPD (*,***,*** bytes) C2KPANL.UPD (***,*** bytes) C2KDRIV.UPD (***,*** bytes) Please refer to the latest information on the support website for asterisks. Preparations for updating firmware Copy the extracted firmware files to the root directory of a USB memory device. * Do not change the name of the extracted files. * Use a USB device formatted in FAT or FAT32 (mass storage class). Program Update Procedures 1. Please set your CDJ player in status below. Power off No device connected (USB memory, SD Card, LINK or Computer) No disc loaded 2. Turn on the power on your CDJ Player by pressing the both "USB" and "RELOOP/EXIT" buttons. (Continue holding both buttons until the Pioneer DJ logo screen disappears.) 3. A message [Connect the USB storage device to the USB port] appears on the display. Then release both buttons. 4. Insert the USB memory device containing the firmware files into the USB port. Updating will start. The display shows [NOW UPDATING] that message blinks. Also [AUTO BEAT LOOP] LED blinks. (If the version of your firmware is the latest one, updating will not start. The display shows "Connect USB storage device into top USB port.") z Do not turn the power off during the update. z The update will be completed in approx. 2 minutes. z A following message appears when the update is completed. [Firmware update is completed. Turn the power off before using. ]. 5. Turn the power off and remove the USB memory device after the update has been completed. If the progress indicator does not progress during updating and update completed message does not appear after 5 minutes, or if the message [MAIN firmware update failed], [GUI firmware update failed], [PANEL firmware update failed] or [SERVO DRIVE firmware update failed] appears, contact our customer support center.

Description Download firmware for your CDJ-400, making it functional as a controller for use with DJ Software Application. This firmware will also improve the Audio Transfer when using the internal sound card with ASIO drivers, along with various other known bugs. You'll only need this firmware if your CDJ-400 is lower than version 1.33. From version 1.8.2 (September 2008) on, you can now control Serato Scratch LIVE from the transport and buttons of the CDJ-400. Scratch Live will also send the track information directly to your CDJ-400 display. We recommend updating your CDJ-400 with the latest firmware to take full advantage of Serato's update (full details of the update are available here ). Simply follow the steps below: 1. CHECK WHICH VERSION OF THE CDJ-400 YOU OWN Turn your CDJ-400 on Press and hold the RELOOP/EXIT button for 10 seconds. The system version will show on the display. If your firmware version is 1.33 or above, you do not need to update your CDJ-400. To exit, turn the power off. 2. DOWNLOAD THE FIRMWARE You can download the firmware and use either a USB memory device or your CD-R/RW drive to burn the firmware to a CD-R Disc. You can then use the USB stick or disc to update your CDJ-400. If you use a USB memory device, it should be formatted in FAT or FAT32 If you use a CD-R or CD-RW, you will require a CD-R/RW Drive, CD-R/RW Writing Software and one unused CD-R or a CD-RW disc. Double click your downloaded file to extract the firmware. Specify a folder for the extracted files. If you don't specify a folder, the extracted files will be placed in the same folder as the downloaded file. After extraction, you will see the file: CDJ400.HEX File size: 4.21MB (4,424,180 bytes) Copy this file to your USB memory device or disc (it is essential that you do not change the file name). 3. UPDATE THE FIRMWARE ON YOUR CDJ-400 With power turned OFF, remove any connected USB memory or computer, and confirm that no discs are loaded. While simultaneously holding the TIME MODE/AUTO CUE button and RELOOP/EXIT buttons, set power to ON. When "DOWNLOAD" appears in the display, remove your fingers from the TIME MODE/AUTO CUE button and RELOOP/EXIT button. Insert the USB memory or disc containing the firmware update file. The progress of the update will be indicated in the display. Do not turn power OFF while the update process is continuing Update will be completed in about 1 minute. When the update is completed, the display will show "COMPLETE." If the update was performed from USB memory, turn the power OFF before disconnecting the USB memory. If the update was performed from a disc, the disc holder will open automatically to eject the disc; remove the disc and turn the power off. This completes the update procedure. Description This update will improve the Master Tempo performance, improve readability of dirty or scratched discs and improves loop performance and better disc recognition (CD-R – CDDA). You'll only need to update your CDJ-800MK2 if it has a System Version lower than 2.10. Just follow the five easy steps below: 1. CHECK WHICH VERSION OF THE CDJ-800MK2 YOU OWN Turn the player on. Press down the RELOOP/EXIT button for 10 seconds. The system version will appear on the player's display. Remember, you'll only need to update if your System has a Version lower than 2.10. To return the CDJ-800MK2 player to its normal operating state, turn the player off and then on again. 2. DOWNLOAD THE FIRMWARE The file is 2.68MB and was released on 15 April 2008 If your browser does not automatically start the download process, be sure that your browser or security software is set to allow downloading of files from this web site. You can also right-click the link and select “Save Target As” to start the download process. 3. CHECK THE FIRMWARE FILE Check the size (2.68MB) of the downloaded firmware file CDJ800MK2_V2.10.iso by right-clicking on the file and selecting Properties. (Download again if the file size is incorrect.) 4. CREATE THE UPDATE DISC It is important to use a good quality branded blank CD-R disc or completely erased CD-RW disc. Create the update disc by burning the file to a CD-R or CD-RW disc. Burn the file to the disc using disc-at-once mode (2x Speed) and ISO 9660 CD-ROM file system format. (The player will not recognize the update disc and the update cannot be performed if the file is not burned correctly to the disc.) Make sure that the disc session is closed / the disc is finalised. 5. UPDATE YOUR DEVICE Update the firmware using the CD-R or CD-RW disc containing the firmware file. Perform the update following the procedure given in the PDF CDJ-800MK2 update instructions. Note: Do not turn off or disconnect power, or remove a disc while update is in progress.
